title: activity — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by `ActivityServiceProvider::boot()` so any FormRequest / Data class can validate activity + retention payloads. Rules run at the request boundary before observers fire — clean 422 errors on invalid input.

rules.name: valid_retention_days
rules.description: Verifies retention_days is between 30 and 730 (inclusive). Nullable — the rule only fires when a value is passed. Entitlement gate (retention_days > 90 requires `activity_retention_extended`) is enforced at the policy layer, NOT here — a rule cannot enforce entitlements.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be between 30 and 730 days.

rules.name: valid_log_name
rules.description: Verifies log_name matches one of the registered names in data/log-names-catalog.json (loaded from the module's cache). Enforces the format constraint too — alphanumeric + dash only, starting with a letter. Nullable — the rule only fires on non-null values.
rules.default_message: The log name ':value' is not a registered feed. Registered feeds: :registered_names.

rules.name: valid_causer_type
rules.description: Verifies causer_type is one of `Stackra\User\Models\User`, `Stackra\User\Models\PlatformUser`, `Stackra\Identity\Models\ServiceAccount`, or the sentinel `Stackra\Observability\Activity\Causers\SystemCauser`. Rejects arbitrary class names. Runs at write time (not read time — read filters trust the DB values).
rules.default_message: The causer type ':value' is not supported. Accepted: User, PlatformUser, ServiceAccount, System.

rules.name: valid_subject_type
rules.description: Verifies subject_type is a class registered with `LogsActivity` (via the vendor morph map). Reads the morph map at boot; refreshed on `config:cache` clear. Refuses arbitrary class names — a subject that doesn't compose LogsActivity has no business appearing on an activity row.
rules.default_message: The subject type ':value' does not participate in activity logging.

rules.name: valid_batch_uuid
rules.description: Verifies batch_uuid is a valid v4 UUID. Not FK-constrained (there's no batches table — batch_uuid is a request-scoped grouping key), so the rule enforces the shape only.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid v4 UUID.

usage_from_data_class: The CreateActivityRetentionPolicyData / UpdateActivityRetentionPolicyData / ActivityExportRequestData spatie-data classes register these rules via their `rules()` method OR compose them via Spatie validation attributes when the arg shape matches.
